title: Bot messages incorrectly trigger silence logic
symptom: The bot records its own responses as human interactions, triggering silence logic after the first reply.
not_this_if: Messages are sent only from the lawyer's WhatsApp Web
root_cause: Both bot messages and manual messages from the lawyer have the same 'fromMe' and 'source' values, making it impossible to distinguish between them based solely on the webhook payload.
fix_steps: Tag your bot's outgoing messages with a custom identifier before sending them.
Add a unique prefix or invisible character to the message body, or store the message ID of every message your bot sends in your database.
When the webhook fires with fromMe: true, check if that message ID already exists in your records.
If it does, skip the timestamp update; if it doesn't, trigger the silence logic.
agent_instruction: Implement a custom identifier for bot messages to distinguish them from human messages.
